danger_signals.visual_form: Creatures depicted as if 'jumped from the pages of the Monster Manual' with distinct visual designs per creature type
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate visual threat identification; players recognize danger types by established monster aesthetics

danger_signals.visual_form: Three sprite versions (short-, medium-, long-range) increase in visual prominence as threats approach
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity danger escalation; larger sprites indicate imminent combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen switches from first-person exploration to top-down dimetric projection view
danger_signals.meaning: Shift to tactical threat environment; dangerous encounter initiated

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental setting depicted in exploration scenes
danger_signals.meaning: Undead and dark entity presence; thematic danger zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Player's primary view in non-combat areas; rectangle display in top-left showing safe navigable space
safety_signals.meaning: Non-hostile exploration mode; safe travel and interaction possible

safety_signals.visual_form: Conversation options displayed through menu interface; character portrait dialogs
safety_signals.meaning: Peaceful negotiation state; neutral or allied contact possible

safety_signals.visual_form: Phlan port city and 'settled part of the city' as designated safe zones
safety_signals.meaning: Civilized, protected territory; reduced random encounter threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Character management, trading, spell-casting menus remain available
safety_signals.meaning: Non-combat state stability; time for preparation and resource management

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present in settled parts of Phlan; movement through cleared territories

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Monster type identification through sprite design (Monster Manual aesthetic)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different creature types serve different evil leader roles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupy unsettled surroundings of Phlan; concentrated around final evil leader location
Represented through quests and NPCs in settled city areas; they enlist player party for territorial clearing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Settled city areas with reduced visual threat density; safe exploration environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced random encounter frequency; NPC availability for peaceful interaction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unsettled areas filled with monster sprites; higher visual danger saturation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Frequent random encounters; monsters immediately engage party; combat-centric interaction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental concentration of high-threat monster types; visual escalation toward final encounter location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progressively stronger enemy bands; tactical combat difficulty increases; party must engage elite forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPC portrait and dialogue reaction changes based on party moral philosophy choices
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC attitudes shift; some refuse interaction with opposing alignment; quest restrictions apply

environmental_storytelling.example: Phlan's dichotomy: settled city center vs. marauding-infested surroundings visually represents the conflict state
Monster type concentration in specific areas indicates faction strongholds; progression toward final evil leader location creates visual quest narrative
Thematic undead/dark entity presence communicated through location visuals; reinforces danger type expectations

environmental_storytelling.technique: Sprite design fidelity to source material
environmental_storytelling.example: Monster Manual aesthetic in creature depiction links game world to established D&D lore; visual consistency reinforces world legitimacy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Single melee/ranged attack per round; standard combat positioning
Multiple attacks per round; 'sweep' ability to hit multiple low-level creatures simultaneously; visual dominance in combat
Spell-casting availability displayed through menu; action delay for casting vs. immediate fighter attacks
Back-stab capability triggered through tactical positioning; visual confirmation of positioning success
Adjacent enemies gain opportunity attacks; visual indication of vulnerability during retreat
Requires bandaging action from ally; visual incapacitation state; death risk escalation
Immediate combat engagement per encounter table patterns; no negotiation default unless player initiates parley

notes: Pool of Radiance uses D&D ruleset mechanics translated to visual language. Danger/safety signals rely heavily on perspective shifts (first-person vs. isometric) and sprite proximity scaling. Faction identity is communicated through Monster Manual-sourced aesthetics and territorial distribution rather than explicit sigils. World state manifests through environmental settlement vs. corruption dichotomy and monster type/density variation. The game's primary manifestation layer challenge is translating turn-based mechanics into visual threat communication and alignment-based NPC reaction differentiation.
